propositions: Transition authorship signing from signing raw canonical event bytes to signing an in-toto Statement payload whose subject binds {event_uuid, content_hash}.
authorship_content_hash computes SHA-256 lowercase hex over the canonical event JSON with the author_sig key excluded so the signature never covers itself.
sign_event_authorship builds the in-toto Statement and signs its canonical JSON as the DSSE payload under the pinned authorship namespace.

verify_event_authorship parses the DSSE payload, checks subject name equals event uuid and digest equals content hash, then verifies the signature against the identity's in-band keys, returning a non-verified Verdict (never raising) on any parse/shape/mismatch failure.
The low-level sign_authorship/verify_authorship SSHSIG-over-DSSE primitive is preserved unchanged so KEY-op signing over keyop_payload bytes still verifies.
Changes are scoped to authorship.py only, with consumer cutover and the create_placeholder alias deferred to a later story.
